mechanism_narrative: Low and stable unemployment indicates a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 8% for a sustained period (e.g., 12 months) but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat on average over that period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month increase in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, signaling strong corporate revenues and driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% for three consecutive months, but the S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of those triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is low and the federal funds rate is also low, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment that supports equity valuations by reducing the cost of capital and increasing the present value of future earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth below 2% for two consecutive quarters, yet S&P 500 60-day returns are negative on average during that period.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in housing starts during a post-recession recovery signals renewed economic activity and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as construction and related industries benefit.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows >10% MoM growth during a period officially declared as post-recession recovery, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in non-farm payroll employment growth indicates a weakening labor market, which will likely lead to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth rate falls below 0.1%
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls below 0.1% for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects a healthy manufacturing sector and overall economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows positive year-over-year growth for 12 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative on average over that period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and declining unemployment signals a robust economic expansion, which boosts corporate profitability and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND UNRATE 3-month average change < -0.1pp
failure_condition: If both GDPC1 growth and UNRATE decline conditions are met for two consecutive quarters, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 30% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary tightening, which typically dampens economic activity and increases the cost of capital, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances.
